question: Compare the NuScale pressurizer design with a conventional separate pressurizer vessel.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing real-time pressure and level data to control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section outlines the inspection and testing requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components and actuation valves in the NuScale reactor. It specifies that these valves are exercised during cold shutdown conditions, in accordance with ASME OM Code, and that comprehensive testing—including position verification and a one-time in-situ heat removal test—will be performed to ensure proper function and compliance with technical specifications.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary and the SG tubes and welds contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details how these components are integrated within the module, outlines their boundaries between primary and secondary systems, and references where further design specifics can be found in other report sections.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component and barrier to fission product release, supporting various reactor systems and constructed according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity throughout the plant's design life. Material specifications for the RPV and its components are selected to maintain safety and compliance with regulatory codes.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and is engineered to prevent fuel design limit violations during all operational conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system piping, specifically focusing on steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the primary degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that the NuScale design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not introduce additional risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear remaining the most likely degradation mechanism and secondary side corrosion risks assessed as low.

edges.description: Section A identifies that the NuScale design integrates the pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel along with the steam generators, while Section B provides specific details about the pressurizer's integral configuration and its separation from the coolant region, directly illustrating how the NuScale pressurizer differs from a conventional separate vessel.

Section A describes the integrated design of the NuScale Power Module, which includes the pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B details inspection requirements for components such as the DHRS, which are also part of the integrated system described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system, noting that the pressurizer is integrated within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B specifies the detailed design and configuration of this integral pressurizer, including its location and separation from the coolant region.

Section A describes the integrated design of the NuScale reactor vessel, which includes the pressurizer as an internal component, while Section B outlines inspection and classification requirements for components such as valves and piping, both of which are relevant to understanding how the integrated pressurizer design differs from conventional separate pressurizer vessels in terms of system configuration and regulatory compliance.

Section B specifies that the pressurizer is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel described in Section A, connecting the design of the pressurizer directly to the reactor vessel's configuration.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system, mentioning the inclusion of an integral pressurizer, while Section B gives specific design details about how the pressurizer is integrated into the reactor vessel, thus specifying the general description from Section A.

Section A describes the NuScale RCS design as including an integral pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B outlines inspection and classification requirements for RCS components, both providing details relevant to comparing the integrated pressurizer approach with conventional separate pressurizer vessels.

Section A and Section B both describe that the NuScale design integrates the pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), unlike conventional designs with a separate pressurizer vessel, thus providing consistent information about the integrated configuration.

Section A specifies that the NuScale design integrates the pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B provides a general summary confirming that the RCS includes an integral pressurizer, thus Section A provides detailed design information that supports and elaborates on the general system description in Section B.

Section A describes the integrated design of the NuScale reactor coolant system, explicitly noting that the pressurizer is contained within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B reiterates that the pressurizer is integrated into the RPV, contrasting with conventional designs where the pressurizer is a separate vessel.

Section A specifies that the NuScale design incorporates the pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B provides a summary confirming the RCS includes an integral pressurizer, thus Section A provides detailed component design that supports and elaborates on the summary in Section B.

Section A describes the reactor vessel as containing the pressurizer within a single vessel, while Section B specifies that the reactor coolant system includes an integral pressurizer as part of the reactor pressure vessel, connecting both sections through the design integration of the pressurizer.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system, noting the inclusion of an integral pressurizer, while Section B specifies the detailed design and configuration of this integral pressurizer within the reactor vessel, highlighting how it differs from conventional separate pressurizer vessels.

Section A describes the NuScale reactor coolant system's integral pressurizer design, while Section B outlines inspection requirements for components (including valves) within the same system, connecting them through their focus on RCS component design and regulatory compliance.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale reactor coolant system, mentioning the inclusion of an integral pressurizer, while Section B specifies the detailed components within the reactor pressure vessel, including the pressurizer and steam generators, thus Section B provides specific details that elaborate on Section A's general overview.

Section B describes the integrated design of the NuScale Power Module, including the pressurizer as part of the reactor pressure vessel, while Section A discusses the reactor coolant system piping and notes differences in steam generator tube design, both providing context for comparing the integrated NuScale pressurizer with conventional separate pressurizer vessels.
